explanations.4: The backslash before # is escaping the hash symbol so it's treated as a literal character in the search pattern, not a special regex character.
explanations.5: The square brackets [\s] match any whitespace character (spaces, tabs, newlines), and the * means 'zero or more of them'; the parentheses capture whatever comes after for later use.
explanations.6: This is the plain text pattern to search for when folding code, while foldStartRegex on line 7 is the more flexible pattern-matching version that finds the same thing.
explanations.7: The .* at the end captures any characters after 'region' into a group, letting the editor extract the region name even if it has extra spaces or special formatting.
